explanation:
Unit conversions are the foundation of science, engineering, and everyday life. The metric system (SI) is the global standard. The US and some territories maintain the imperial system.

## How It's Calculated

oz × 28.35 = g. Free and fast.

This calculator applies the formula with the values you enter, checking typical ranges and alerting you to values outside the reasonable range.

## Final Notes

This calculation is a reference guide. For critical decisions (financial, medical, legal), consult with a qualified professional. Values are current as of 2026 and reviewed regularly.

faq.q: How do you convert ounces to grams?
faq.a: Multiply the number of ounces by 28.35. For example, 1 oz × 28.35 = 28.35 grams.

faq.q: What is the standard conversion factor for ounces to grams?
faq.a: The standard conversion is 1 ounce = 28.35 grams. This is the internationally accepted factor.

faq.q: Can I convert grams to ounces with this calculator?
faq.a: Yes, you can select 'Grams to ounces' from the conversion direction dropdown. Divide grams by 28.35.

faq.q: Is this calculator accurate enough for baking and cooking?
faq.a: Yes, the results are accurate within ±5%. Precise enough for most cooking and baking recipes.

faq.q: Do I need to download anything to use this calculator?
faq.a: No. This is a free online tool. Just enter your value and select your conversion direction.
Your data is never saved. All calculations happen in your browser. We don't send any information to our servers.

faq.q: When was this calculator last updated?
faq.a: The conversion formula is fixed and doesn't require updates. The calculator is maintained regularly.

faq.q: Can I use this for metric conversions in chemistry?
faq.a: Yes. This calculator works for any ounce-to-gram conversion, whether for cooking, chemistry, or other applications.
